untranslatable_reason: Signal requires options pricing data (call_price, put_price, synthetic position valuation, cost_of_carry calculation, and mispricing magnitude in basis points). None of these are available in the allowed indicator set, which contains only spot price, technical indicators, and volatility measures. This is a pure derivatives arbitrage signal that cannot be expressed using only spot price technical indicators.
translation_notes: NATENBERG_DRY_100_0 is a conversion/reversal arbitrage strategy requiring real-time options chain data and cost-of-carry modeling. The signal logic depends on: (1) synthetic price = call - put, (2) comparing underlying price vs synthetic price, (3) calculating locked-in profit margins accounting for financing costs and transaction costs. The source text references a .25 profit differential that is 'locked in' before execution—this requires options pricing mechanics unavailable in a spot-only technical indicator framework. To implement this signal, the backtest engine would need: call_bid, call_ask, put_bid, put_ask, risk_free_rate, dividend_yield, and days_to_expiration indicators.
